msgs.text:
Turn 14 — the lower Y4 seed toggles λ; degree 16 dies I used only the three files attached to this turn. The original question is still whether the profinite one-relator groups ΓS,ΓH, defined from rS,rH, are isomorphic over the fixed quotient G, i.e. whether there is a continuous isomorphism Φ:ΓS→ΓH with πH∘Φ=πS.

The new result is:

The degree-16 obstruction candidate dies once the lower I9-base torsor is included.So Turn 11’s codimension-one high-torsor obstruction is not a genuine obstruction to ΓS≅GΓH.

Recall the residual equation from the prior sweep:S(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q,X=1+A,Y=1+B,with target X2=1+A2. The previous turns had reduced all surviving obstruction tests to this residual mod-2, s=1, Ef-piece. Turn 5 produced an I9 -base lift Y0(8),Y1(8),q(8), and Turn 9 cleared the residual tower through I16, leaving degree 16 as the first genuinely nonlinear 8+8 layer.

Turn 11 then found that the high-correction torsor alone has image of rank 35 inside the 36-dimensional quotientL16/im∂15≅L8[2].

The missing functional was λ=coeff ((AB+BA)4)[2]+coeff(B8)[2],and the fixed-base degree-16 class had λ=1. But Turn 13 showed that the lower I9 -base torsor already has rank-one shadow on this missing square coordinate: the degree-4 seed Y0↦Y0Y4 survives to an I9 -base and contributes the B8 square shadow.The remaining question was whether this shadow survives the full continuation through degrees 9,…,15, or whether it is cancelled by forced high corrections. It survives.

Start from the Turn-5 I9 -base lift and postmultiply Y0 by Y4. The raw seed produces no degree-5 or degree-6 error; its first new tails occur in degrees 7 and 8. They are killed by the actual restricted correctionsK6:=(X,(X,Y4)),K7:=(Y,(Y,(X,Y4))).

Thus the lower-deformed baseY0=Y0(8)Y4 (X,(X,Y4))(Y,(Y,(X,Y4))),Y1=Y1(8),q=q(8)satisfiesS(Y0,Y1,q)≡X2 (modI9).

This is the concrete continuation of the Y4 survivor seed from Turn 13.

For this lower-deformed I9 -base, the residual tails in degrees 9,…,15 are again killable by high corrections. Using actual restricted representatives generated by commutators and squares, the high correction mapd=8⨁14(3Ld)⟶L9⊕L10⊕⋯⊕L15 has full target rank 56+105+186+346+630+1179+2182=4684.The lower-deformed residual vector lies in its image. After applying one high solution, the first remaining term is homogeneous degree 16.

Now compute the degree-16 square coordinates inL16/im∂15≅L8[2].

With the same two λ-coordinates used in Turn 11,((AB+BA)4)[2],(B8)[2],the fixed Turn-11 base hasλ(Ω16)=1,whereas the lower-deformed Y4 -base hasλ(Ω16)=0.

Equivalently,Λ16low(Y4)=1.So the scalar continuation map from the lower-base torsor is nonzero:imΛ16low=F2.This is decisive for degree 16. Turn 11 showed that the high-correction image is exactly a codimension-one hyperplane annihilated by λ. The lower Y4 -deformation moves the actual degree-16 class into that hyperplane. Once the class lies in kerλ, the high torsor kills the remaining degree-16 square component.

Therefore there exists a residual section-twisted lift withS(Y0,Y1,q)≡X2 (modI17).Then degree 17 is odd, so the earlier primitive-boundary argument applies: once the preceding even layer is solved, any first degree-17 tail is a boundary in[A,L16]+[B,L16].Thus degree 17 also clears, and the residual congruence improves toS(Y0,Y1,q)≡X2 (modI18).The structural lesson is important. Degree 16 looked like the first serious non-isomorphism candidate because the high torsor missed exactly one square direction. But that missed direction is supplied by the lower I9 -base torsor, already visible at the Y4 seed. So the obstruction was real in the fixed high gauge, but not invariant under the full lower-plus-high correction problem.

This does not prove ΓS≅GΓH. It proves that the strongest obstruction candidate found so far, the Turn-11 degree-16 λ-class, is false after including the full lower-base freedom.

ESTABLISHED: The lower I9 -base scalar continuation is nonzero. In particular, the Y0↦Y0Y4 survivor seed toggles the Turn-11 missing functional λ. Hence the degree-16 class can be moved into imΘ16, killed by high corrections, and the odd-layer argument then clears degree 17. The residual equation is solvable throughS(Y0,Y1,q)≡X2 (modI18).

GAP: This still is not a full construction of an over-G isomorphism ΓS≅GΓH. The next possible genuine obstruction is degree 18, with square quotientL18/im∂17≅L9[2],dimL9=56.

NEXT: Build the degree-18 combined torsor, not just a fixed high-gauge tail. It must include the lower-base transgression together with high corrections, because degree 16 showed that the high torsor alone can miss a direction that the lower base supplies.[DEG16-LOWER-TOGGLED-DEG17-CLEARED]
